w:r.w:t: Infusions, Liqueurs, Syrups & Dosing Charts — Digitized from the Notebook

This document covers the rest of the recipe book: infused spirits, liqueurs and cordials, syrups and extracts, and the master flavor-dosing chart. The canned RTD cocktails (John Daly, Cherry Limeade, Mango Collins, Grape Popsicle, Freshkosh, Swifty Swig, Blu) have their own dedicated document with exact 5.0% ABV math and are only referenced here where they connect to a base spirit.
A few figures in the original notes were smudged, crossed out, or otherwise hard to read. Those spots are flagged in place — worth double-checking against the original page before scaling up a batch.

Test 1 came out crispy and was rated “Good”; Test 2 (bigger batch) was crispy and rated “OK.” Follow-up note: at production scale the crispy bacon didn't infuse as well as hoped — next trial swaps in floppy (less-cooked) bacon instead.
1 case ≈ 8290 mL vodka; ~8.25 mL color per case
This entry's production math was hard to read cleanly in the notebook (smudged figures) — re-derive from the base ratio or check the original page before batching at scale.
Bulk/commercial-scale formula — quantities partly illegible in the original; treat as a starting point and verify before a full batch.
Conversion note in the book: about 7.5 g fresh ginger ≈ 1 inch piece.
This recipe is crossed out in the notebook (old production numbers: 112 oz per 10 lb bucket, 552 lb per bucket, etc.) — marked as no longer in use.
1 lb per medium bottle of base spirit
Freshkosh (grapefruit-lime gin) is the base for the canned Freshkosh RTD — see the RTD Recipes document for the full 5-gallon batch and exact ABV.
Macerate rhubarb + sugar for 1 day, then add the gin. Steep 1–4 weeks.
recipe header only — detailed amounts not fully legible in these notes
The notebook also has bigger-batch multipliers (roughly 3×–6× scale-ups) — some of the scaled weights were hard to read cleanly, so double-check against the original before batching large.
3, plus 3 Tbsp bitter orange peel — let stand 48 hours
32 (dose 4 g, stepping up to 5 g right before switching batches)
4 cups water + 2½ cups sugar, to taste
Steep 2 weeks. Conversion note: ~7.5 g fresh ginger ≈ 1 inch piece.
1 cup cocoa powder (130 g) : 1 cup sugar (200 g)
Method: mix cocoa powder + water + sugar and heat on a hot plate first. Wait until the mixture cools before adding alcohol — otherwise the alcohol will evaporate off.
~8.2 gallons of 93%, cut to 60% with ~4.5 gallons water
Double everything for a bigger batch.
Boil cider + juice 5–10 minutes. Add sugar and the 8 cinnamon sticks, let cool completely, then stir in the GNS. Add 2 fresh cinnamon sticks before bottling.
Combine the steeped lemon-peel base with the water/sugar mixture.
Combine and let sit for one month.
same as Chocolate Liqueur, made without alcohol
blended in at roughly 4 parts chocolate mix : 3 parts cold brew
Ratios noted as approximate in the book — taste and adjust.
toast the same mix used for Pumpkin Spice Vodka before combining
same botanicals list as the Dry Vermouth above
Substitute this trio for the 4 cups sparkling grape juice used in the dry version — the notebook flags that it still needs the full spice/botanical list from the dry recipe too.
Shake well and let sit overnight.
Steep the vodka on the mint first, then add sugar and water.
Cumin, Worcestershire, celery salt, pepper, green hot sauce, lemon juice
Noted as a Mother's Day batch — seasonal, made with spring pine tips.
Mix directly in the pourer bottle.
Per-bottle dosing (750 mL base) for the flavored-vodka product line:
4 mL vanilla extract + 2 mL fresh vanilla
4 mL — either Amoretti extract or Northwest Extracts (NW) extract (not both; for a clear spirit base such as rum or vodka)
2 mL (Blood Orange extract — distinct product from the Amoretti Blood Orange Sanguine used in Triple Sec)
4 mL mango extract, dosed into Habanero vodka

Raspberry case scaling (12 bottles): 72 mL raspberry extract, 24 Tbsp (1½ cups) simple syrup, 12 drops red food color. (This is the Raspberry per-bottle dose — 6 mL extract, 2 Tbsp simple syrup, 1 drop red — multiplied by 12; it is not a generic rule for every flavor.)
Peppermint 8-gallon batch (8 gallons vodka): ⅔ cup peppermint flavor, 5 cups sugar, 40 drops red food color.

Blood Orange here is a standalone extract dosed straight into vodka for its own Blood Orange Vodka on the flavored-vodka line — it is a different product from the Amoretti Blood Orange Sanguine used as the flavor booster in the Triple Sec recipe (Part 3); do not conflate the two.
Blood Orange scale-up: base dose is 2 mL per 750 mL bottle; a 6-gallon batch needs 80 mL of Blood Orange flavor, and a 4-gallon batch needs 45 mL (≈ 3.5 Tbsp).

Mango Habanero is dosed into a Habanero-infused vodka base rather than plain vodka; the notebook doesn’t give a separate Habanero Vodka infusion recipe on this page, so that base recipe still needs to be tracked down.
Coconut Cream Liqueur: 6 mL coconut extract per bottle.
Banana Cream Liqueur: 6 mL banana extract per bottle.
Pineapple Cream Liqueur: 7 mL pineapple extract per bottle.
GNS = grain neutral spirit, dosed at 93% ABV throughout this book unless noted otherwise.

A typed reference sheet in the notebook cross-checks the RTD percentages: Mango Collins vodka = 12.64% of total volume, Cherry Limeade cherry vodka = 14.29%, Grape Popsicle cran vodka = 13.89% — these line up with the ABV figures already calculated in the separate RTD Recipes document.

Several bulk/commercial-scale entries (Cherry Vodka, Cranberry Vodka, Amaretto multipliers, Coffee Liqueur case batches) had partly illegible figures in the original — flagged inline above; re-verify against the physical notebook before scaling production.
The “Squirts of Flavoring Per Bottle” page also has the proofing math for cutting 93% GNS down to 40% ABV to yield 9,000 mL of finished vodka: 1,023 g of 93% GNS + 1,355 g water (2,378 g total).
